questions.question: How many numbers should you review every Friday in your weekly financial review?
questions.explanation: The Friday Five: Cash Balance + 13-Week Status, Net New MRR, Outstanding Invoices, CAC Payback Period, and Gross Margin. Five numbers, 20-30 minutes, every Friday.

questions.question: What is the most common finance mistake among solo founders?

options.text: Not having a consistent weekly review routine

questions.explanation: The most common mistake isn't bad accounting — it's not having a routine. Founders check numbers when worried, ignore them when things feel good, and spend enormous mental energy on anxiety that a simple weekly ritual would eliminate.

questions.question: How often should you conduct a deep financial review beyond the weekly Friday Five?
questions.explanation: Monthly: 2 hours for a deeper review. The weekly Friday Five takes 20-30 minutes. Monthly reviews add deeper analysis of trends, forecasting, and strategic financial planning.

questions.question: Solo businesses with a consistent weekly financial review spend 70% less time worrying about money.
questions.explanation: True. Not because their numbers are better, but because they're never surprised. You can only fix what you're watching. What you're not watching becomes a crisis.

questions.question: Design your Friday Five financial review. Where will you find each of the 5 numbers? What red flags will trigger immediate action? When will you schedule your first review?
questions.aiPrompt: Evaluate whether the response maps each of the 5 metrics to a specific data source (bank portal, Stripe, spreadsheet), defines at least one red flag threshold that triggers action, and commits to a specific day and time for the first review.
